concerns.text: FR-006 requires regression models to predict 'the magnitude of deviation from nominal alpha (|p - α|)' AND 'the log-transformed p-value distribution'. Task T027b covers the log-transformed distribution, and T027 covers the regression on deviation magnitude. However, T027's description focuses on 'predict the magnitude of deviation... using log(sample size)...' but does not explicitly mention the output of 'regression coefficients (beta) and p-values' as required by FR-006's second clause, nor does it explicitly state it models the deviation metric |p - α|. While T027b handles the log-p-value regression, the specific requirement to model the deviation magnitude |p - α| is only implicitly covered by T027's 'regression analysis' description without explicit confirmation of the target variable. To ensure full coverage, T027 should explicitly state it models |p - α| and outputs beta/p-values.

FR-002 and SC-001 require the system to classify outcomes as Type I/II errors and measure against theoretical alpha. Task T021 implements the counting logic. However, SC-001 specifically states: 'The observed Type I error rate... is measured against the theoretical nominal alpha level to validate the simulation engine.' There is no explicit task to perform this specific validation comparison (calculating the difference between observed and theoretical 0.05 and reporting it as a validation metric) before or after the main simulation loop. T015 tests the logic, but no task explicitly performs the 'validation against theoretical nominal alpha' as a distinct step or output artifact required by SC-001.

SC-004 requires measuring 'The reduction rate of Type II error rates (increase in power)... where success is defined as the observed power curve matching the theoretical power curve within a negligible mean absolute error.' While T026 aggregates error rates and T027 performs regression, there is no explicit task to calculate the 'theoretical power curve' (which requires a power calculation function based on effect size and sample size) and then compute the 'mean absolute error' between the observed and theoretical curves. The current tasks cover the observed data but miss the specific task of generating the theoretical baseline and computing the MAE metric required to satisfy SC-004's success definition.
